title: Gaultheria rhombica
intro: Gaultheria rhombica, also known as Himalayan Gaultheria or Himalayan Wintergreen, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in relieving pain, reducing fever, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Gaultheria rhombica include methyl salicylate, a compound similar to aspirin, along with other compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. In traditional herbal medicine, Gaultheria rhombica is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used as a flavoring agent due to its sweet, wintergreen-like flavor.
taxonomy: Gaultheria rhombica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. This plant is native to Japan and is also known as Japanese wintergreen. The genus Gaultheria belongs to the order Ericales. Gaultheria rhombica is further classified as a member of the subfamily Vaccinioideae. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Ericaceae family.

parts: Gaultheria rhombica, also known as the Himalayan tea, is a species of flowering plant native to the Himalayas and other mountain ranges. The medicinal parts of Gaultheria rhombica are primarily the leaves, stems, and roots. The leaves and stems of this plant are used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The roots of Gaultheria rhombica are also used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including phenolic acids and flavonoids.
